questions.question: What is the IUCN conservation status of the Red-breasted parakeet that was recently spotted at National Institute of Technology (NIT) Patna?
questions.explanation: Two pairs of red-breasted parakeets were spotted on the National Institute of Technology (NIT) Patna campus, exciting birdwatchers and highlighting local biodiversity. These birds are listed as Near Threatened on the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Red List. This is the first confirmed sighting of red-breasted parakeets in Bihar. Scientific name of the species is Psittacula alexandri, also called the moustached parakeet. They are native to Southeast Asian countries and easily identified by the large red patch on their breast.

questions.question: Shingles disease, that was recently seen in news, is caused by which agent?
questions.explanation: A recent study found that the shingles vaccine can reduce the risk of death from dementia. Shingles is a viral infection that causes a painful skin rash. It is caused by the varicella-zoster virus, the same virus responsible for chickenpox. After chickenpox, the virus stays dormant in nerve cells and can reactivate when immunity becomes weak. Shingles usually appears as a painful stripe of blisters on one side of the body, face, or neck. It is not directly contagious but can spread chickenpox to unvaccinated people. It mainly affects people over 50 years or those with weak immune systems. The Shingrix vaccine helps prevent shingles and its complications.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Toy Manufacturing Policy in December 2025?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu launched the Toy Manufacturing Policy 2025 to attract new investments and boost domestic toy production. The policy was released by Chief Minister M. K. Stalin at the Tamil Nadu Investors Conclave held in Madurai. New and expansion projects investing more than ₹50 crore and creating at least 50 jobs are eligible for special incentives within three years. The toy manufacturing sector has been declared a sunrise sector under the Tamil Nadu Industrial Policy (TNIP) 2021. Capital subsidies are provided at 7% in A districts, 10% in B districts, and 12% in C districts over fixed periods.

questions.question: Which state is the host of Rising Global Summit 2025?
questions.explanation: Telangana hosted the Rising Global Summit 2025 in Hyderabad on 8 December. The two-day summit features 27 sessions on energy, Information Technology (IT), healthcare, and startups. Major organisations like World Health Organization (WHO), World Bank, Asian Development Bank (ADB) and United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) are involved. The summit aims to position Telangana as a global growth and innovation hub.

questions.question: India and which country have jointly launched the new agricultural venture ‘Semiophore Ltd.’ to boost sustainable agricultural technologies?
questions.explanation: India and Israel launched a significant new agricultural partnership by creating a joint venture to manufacture and commercialise Indian pheromone-based crop protection technologies for global markets. The announcement was made at the valedictory session of the First International Science & Technology Clusters Conference in New Delhi. ATGC Biotech Pvt. Ltd. (India) and Luxembourg Industries Ltd. (Israel) exchanged licensing agreements to establish Semiophore Ltd., a 50–50 Indo-Israeli joint venture. This marks the first time Indian semiochemical technology is being out-licensed and manufactured in Israel, representing a milestone for both countries.

questions.question: Where was the seventh UN Environment Assembly (UNEA-7) held?
questions.explanation: The seventh United Nations Environment Assembly (UNEA-7) is currently taking place in Nairobi, Kenya. UNEA is the world’s highest-level decision-making body on the environment, created in 2012 following the UN Conference on Sustainable Development (Rio+20) in Brazil. It has universal membership of all 193 UN Member States, with the full involvement of major groups and stakeholders. The theme of United Nations Environment Assembly (UNEA-7) (2025) is “Advancing sustainable solutions for a resilient planet”.

questions.question: Sultanpur National Park is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Sultanpur National Park, located in the Gurgaon district of Haryana about 46 km from Delhi, is witnessing a major increase in migratory birds as winter temperatures drop. The Sultanpur Jheel is a seasonal freshwater wetland whose water levels rise and fall depending on rainfall and seasonal inflows. This shallow lake is mainly replenished by water from the Yamuna River’s Gurgaon canal and by overflow from surrounding agricultural fields. It was designated a Ramsar Site in 2021, recognising its global ecological importance.

questions.question: Which country won the FIH Men’s Junior Hockey World Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: Germany won the International Hockey Federation (FIH) Men’s Junior Hockey World Cup 2025, defeating Spain 3-2 in a shoot-out after a 1-1 draw in regulation time. The final was held at Mayor Radhakrishnan Stadium in Chennai, concluding the 13-day tournament that began on November 28 and was held across Chennai and Madurai. Justus Warweg scored for Germany in the 26th minute, while Nicolas Mustaros equalised for Spain in the 33rd minute. India won the bronze medal, defeating Argentina 4-2, marking their first bronze and adding to their previous tally of two golds and one silver in the tournament.

questions.question: Where was the 70-foot iron statue of football player Lionel Messi unveiled in India?
questions.explanation: A 70-foot iron statue of football legend Lionel Messi has been unveiled at Lake Town, South Dum Dum, Kolkata, West Bengal, by Sree Bhumi Sporting Club. The statue depicts Messi holding the FIFA World Cup trophy; final surface work and area preparations are ongoing. The statue is part of a tribute ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The project received support from West Bengal government authorities.

questions.question: International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialised agency of which organization?
questions.explanation: The Government of India recently showcased the country’s pioneering achievements in rural transformation and development leadership at the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) – India Day event held in Rome, Italy. The event highlighted India’s successful policies, programmes, and innovations aimed at eradicating rural poverty, strengthening livelihoods, and transforming rural economies. International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialised agency of the United Nations. Its primary objective is to eradicate poverty and hunger in rural areas of developing countries by empowering rural communities.
